summary: Backend pricing-formula bug fix verified. /api/menus/preview-hpp now returns recommended_price = hpp/(1-margin) (no double fee), and platform_prices net_received ≈ base_price within rounding tolerance.
passed: POST /api/menus/preview-hpp with empty ingredients/packaging, labor=5000, overhead=2000, margin=60, offline=0 → HPP=7000, recommended_price=17500 (= 7000/0.4 rounded to 500) ✓
Same payload: profit_per_unit=10500 (=17500-7000), profit_margin_pct=60.0% ✓ (formula now uses selling-hpp not net-hpp)
Platform net_received ≈ recommended_price when offline=0: shopeefood=17500 (fee 65%), gofood=17670 (fee 38%), grabfood=17600 (fee 20%) — all within ±500 rounding of 17500 ✓
With offline_price=25000, margin=60: recommended_price still 17500 (margin-based), platform base_price uses 25000 — net_received: 25025/25110/25200 (≈25000) ✓

success_rate.frontend: not tested (cosmetic label only, per request)

notes: Math invariant holds: net_received from each platform_price ≈ base_price (offline_price if set, else recommended_price). Settings has non-default fees (shopeefood=65/gofood=38/grabfood=20) but invariant verified regardless. The fix correctly decouples offline recommendation from platform fee. Note: '/api/sales/preview' endpoint does not exist in the codebase — verified GET /api/sales instead.
